candidates.name: AI Cold Email Subject Variant Generator
candidates.pitch: Buildable in 2-3 weeks using OpenAI's API and a small prompt-engine prompt pack per persona. The wave of AI-powered sales tooling (Smartlead, Instantly) created a pipeline gap: nothing scores subject
candidates.pain: Sales reps waste hours A/B testing subject lines with no signal on which phrasing actually drives replies.

candidates.name: Cold Email Domain Reputation Pulse
candidates.pitch: Detects the user's outbound sending domain from mailto: links and inbox headers, then pings reputation APIs (Google Postmaster, MXToolbox, Spamhaus) to surface a live health badge in Gmail/Outlook. 2-
candidates.pain: Outbound teams don't learn their sending domain reputation has tanked until deliverability collapses and pipeline dries up.

candidates.name: AI Response Hallucination Spotter
candidates.pitch: Runs a side panel over ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini; cross-references claims against search snippets and highlights unsupported sentences in real time. 4-5 weeks using SerpApi + cheap classifiers. Why now: e
candidates.pain: Users paste AI outputs into client work and emails without knowing which factual claims are unsourced or contradicted.

candidates.name: Job Description AI Exposure Index
candidates.pitch: Scores any LinkedIn/Indeed posting on a 0-100 AI exposure scale using task taxonomy + LLM zero-shot classification, with a side-panel rationale. 2 weeks of model work plus a small labeled set. Why now
candidates.pain: Job seekers can't quantify how AI-vulnerable a posting's role is before investing hours tailoring an application.

candidates.name: Supabase RLS Policy Coverage Heatmap
candidates.pitch: Detects a Supabase project from cookies/JWT and walks the public REST schema to flag tables without RLS or with policy gaps. Buildable in 3 weeks using supabase-js in the page context. Why now: Supaba
candidates.pain: Teams ship Supabase projects to staging not knowing which tables lack row-level security policies until an audit finds exposed rows.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: LinkedIn Promotion Velocity Heatmap
candidates.pitch: Aggregates LinkedIn job-posting cadence and promotion-tagged profile updates per company into a 12-week velocity score surfaced as a Sales Navigator overlay. 3-4 weeks of scraping + heuristics. Why no
candidates.pain: AEs burn prospecting cycles on accounts showing no recent hiring momentum in the roles their product actually targets.

candidates.name: Local LLM Page Q&A Sidebar
candidates.pitch: Pairs with WebLLM/transformers.js to run a 1-3B param model in a sidebar; zero data leaves the browser. Buildable in 3-4 weeks since the heavy lifting is inference plumbing. Why now: Chrome's prompt A
candidates.pain: Users want to query any webpage with AI but cloud tools leak their reading habits and clipboard contents to vendors.

candidates.name: Browser Cookie Scope Origin Mapper
candidates.pitch: Parses Set-Cookie attributes on every response and renders an interactive graph of cookie scope across origins, highlighting SameSite=None and cross-domain reads. 2 weeks of pure DevTools work. Why no
candidates.pain: Users can't tell which third-party domains can read cookies set on the site they're currently visiting.
